description: ClawHQ feature backlog — derived from docs/PRODUCT.md build phases

items.title: CLI UI module (src/cli/ui.ts)
items.description: Add chalk + ora deps. Create centralized UI helpers: phase-colored labels (plan=cyan, build=blue, secure=yellow, deploy=green, operate=magenta), status indicators (pass/fail/warn), spinner factory wrapping ora, section headers, and formatError(code, message, hint).
items.groomingNotes: DEPENDENCY ANCHOR — must ship first in Phase 1. Steps: (1) npm install chalk ora; (2) create src/cli/ui.ts; (3) export spinner(text) factory wrapping ora; (4) export phase-colored labels: plan=cyan, build=blue, secure=yellow, deploy=green, operate=magenta; (5) export status indicators: pass/fail/warn with colored symbols; (6) export sectionHeader(title) function; (7) export formatError(code, message, hint) using chalk.red/yellow; (8) verify no existing commands are broken. Acceptance criteria: chalk + ora installed; spinner(), formatError(), sectionHeader(), phase labels all exported and callable; TypeScript compiles clean; existing CLI commands unaffected.

items.title: Grouped CLI help output
items.description: Modify src/cli/index.ts — use Commander configureHelp() to render --help grouped by lifecycle phase (Plan, Build, Secure, Deploy, Operate, Evolve, Fleet, Decommission). All existing command names unchanged — fully backward compatible. Display-only change.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-060 done. Pure Commander configureHelp() display change, ~20 lines. Steps: (1) use program.configureHelp() to override formatHelp; (2) group commands into lifecycle phases: Plan, Build, Secure, Deploy, Operate, Evolve, Decommission; (3) add section headers from ui.ts sectionHeader(); (4) verify non-TTY environments still work; (5) all command names/flags unchanged. AC: clawhq --help shows grouped sections; no behavior change; tsc clean; existing commands unaffected.

items.title: clawhq quickstart command
items.description: Add quickstart command to src/cli/index.ts. Orchestrates init → build → up → smoke with phase-labeled spinners. Flags: --template <id>, --smart, --skip-build, --skip-deploy. Calls existing functions: runWizard() → twoStageBuild() → deployUp() → smoke checks. No new business logic.
items.groomingNotes: HEADLINE FEATURE — orchestrates existing functions, no new business logic. Steps: (1) add 'quickstart' command to src/cli/index.ts; (2) flags: --template <id> (default: personal-assistant), --smart (use AI inference), --skip-build, --skip-deploy; (3) step 1: call runWizard() or non-interactive init with --template; show phase-labeled spinner 'Plan...' (cyan); (4) step 2 (skip if --skip-build): call twoStageBuild(); show spinner 'Build...' (blue); (5) step 3 (skip if --skip-deploy): call deployUp(); show spinner 'Deploy...' (green); (6) step 4: run smoke checks via gateway health endpoint; show spinner 'Verify...' (magenta); (7) on any step failure, show formatError() with actionable hint (not raw stack trace); (8) on success, print friendly summary with access instructions. Note: depends on FEAT-060 for spinner/formatError. Acceptance criteria: clawhq quickstart --template personal-assistant completes end-to-end with phase-labeled spinners; --skip-build and --skip-deploy flags skip those steps; failure at any step shows formatted error with fix hint; --help shows correct usage.

items.title: Friendly error handler (src/cli/errors.ts)
items.description: Create error handler wrapping program.parseAsync() in try/catch. Handle: Docker not installed, daemon not running, no config found, port in use, build fails (no Dockerfile), permission denied on .env. Print styled hints via formatError() from ui.ts instead of raw stack traces.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-060 (formatError) done. Create src/cli/errors.ts wrapping program.parseAsync() in try/catch. Steps: (1) create src/cli/errors.ts; (2) export handleError(err: unknown) mapping error types to formatError() calls; (3) handle 6 error types: Docker not installed (ENOENT on docker), daemon not running (connection refused), no config found (missing openclaw.json), port in use (EADDRINUSE), build fails no Dockerfile, permission denied on .env; (4) wrap program.parseAsync() call in src/cli/index.ts; (5) each error type has code, message, hint fields. AC: each of 6 error types shows styled output not stack trace; unknown errors show generic formatted message; tsc clean; no regression on existing commands.

items.title: First-run experience (src/cli/first-run.ts)
items.description: State file at ~/.clawhq/state.json. Commander preAction hook detects first run (skip for non-TTY, --json, CI env, init/quickstart/--help/version/dashboard). If no ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json and firstRunComplete=false, print one-line hint to run quickstart. Mark complete after first successful init/quickstart.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-060 (ui.ts with spinner/formatError) is done. Steps: (1) create src/cli/first-run.ts; (2) define state file path ~/.clawhq/state.json with firstRunComplete boolean; (3) export checkFirstRun(program: Command): void that registers a preAction hook; (4) hook logic: skip if non-TTY (process.stdout.isTTY === false), skip if --json flag present, skip if CI env (process.env.CI), skip if command is init/quickstart/dashboard/help/version; (5) if ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json does not exist AND firstRunComplete=false, print one-line chalk hint: 'Run clawhq quickstart to set up your agent.'; (6) export markFirstRunComplete(clawhqDir: string): Promise<void> that writes state.json with firstRunComplete=true; (7) call markFirstRunComplete from quickstart and init success paths in their respective CLI modules; (8) import and call checkFirstRun(program) in src/cli/index.ts before program.parseAsync. AC: running clawhq doctor on fresh install (no openclaw.json) prints one-line quickstart hint; hint not shown when already set up; hint not shown in CI (process.env.CI=true); hint not shown with --json flag; hint not shown for init/quickstart commands themselves; markFirstRunComplete suppresses hint on next run; tsc clean.

items.title: Add spinners to existing CLI commands
items.description: Modify action handlers in src/cli/index.ts to add ora spinners: build (per Docker stage), up/down/restart (per deploy step), doctor (while running checks), backup create/restore, update (check + apply), scan. Pattern: const s = spinner('...'); ... s.succeed('...');
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-060 (spinner factory) done. Modify action handlers in src/cli/index.ts to add ora spinners for long-running operations. Steps: (1) import spinner() from src/cli/ui.ts; (2) build command: const s = spinner('Building Stage 1...'); ... s.succeed('Stage 1 complete'); per Docker stage; (3) up/down/restart: spinner per deploy step (compose up, firewall apply, health check); (4) doctor: single spinner while checks run, then show per-check results; (5) backup create/restore: spinner during operation; (6) update: spinners for check and apply phases; (7) scan: spinner while scanning. Pattern is consistent. AC: all listed commands show spinner during operation; spinner.succeed() on success, spinner.fail() on error; non-TTY environments degrade gracefully; no behavior change; tsc clean.

items.title: Non-interactive init for fleet provisioning
items.description: Add --non-interactive flag to clawhq init. Uses template defaults for all values, skipping the wizard. Example: clawhq init --template research-copilot --name agent-2 --non-interactive. Enables scripted multi-agent provisioning.
items.groomingNotes: Fleet provisioning feature — extends existing init command. Steps: (1) add --non-interactive flag to 'clawhq init' command in src/cli/index.ts; (2) add --name <name> flag to init command; (3) add --template <id> flag to init command (may already exist); (4) when --non-interactive is set, skip runWizard() and call generateBundle() directly with template defaults + provided overrides; (5) print single-line success/failure to stdout (no spinners or prompts in non-interactive mode); (6) exit 0 on success, non-zero on failure. Acceptance criteria: clawhq init --template personal-assistant --name agent-2 --non-interactive produces same output as guided wizard with template defaults without prompting; works correctly in a shell loop provisioning multiple agents; exits 0 on success; exits non-zero with error message (not stack trace) on failure; does not print interactive prompts.

items.title: Web dashboard server + layout (Hono/htmx/Pico CSS)
items.description: Create Hono server with @hono/node-server. Server: index.ts (createServer), app.ts (routes), auth.ts (bearer→cookie), sse.ts, ws.ts, context.ts. Frontend: Hono JSX server-rendered + htmx for interactivity + Pico CSS classless styling. Vendor htmx.min.js + pico.min.css in src/ui/static/. Add JSX config to tsconfig.json. Includes sidebar nav organized by lifecycle phase and dashboard home page with agent state card, integration health grid, memory metrics, approval/alert badges. SSE status updates every 5s. This is the foundation all web pages build on.
items.groomingNotes: FOUNDATION — all dashboard pages depend on this. Note: FEAT-068, 069, 070 were planned as separate items (auth, SSE/WS infrastructure, home page) but were merged into this item's scope — their functionality is already described here. Ship as unit with FEAT-071 (API endpoints): a partial dashboard with a broken home page is worse than no dashboard. Server + auth + home + nav + SSE must all land in one sprint. User outcome: 'clawhq dashboard' opens browser to working control panel. Acceptance criteria: Hono server starts on correct port; bearer-to-cookie auth works; sidebar nav renders with lifecycle phase grouping; home dashboard card shows agent state + integration health grid + memory metrics + approval/alert badges; SSE status updates fire every 5s. SPRINT PAIR: sprint FEAT-067 and FEAT-071 together — they are co-dependent and independently useless. Tech stack decided: Hono + htmx + Pico CSS (classless). Vendor htmx.min.js + pico.min.css in src/ui/static/. READY TO SPRINT: grooming is thorough, no external blockers.

items.title: Dashboard API endpoints (v1)
items.description: JSON API returning {ok, data?, error?}. Endpoints: GET /api/v1/status, GET /api/v1/doctor, POST /api/v1/doctor/fix, POST /api/v1/deploy/{up,down,restart} (SSE), POST /api/v1/build (SSE), GET/POST /api/v1/approvals, POST /api/v1/approvals/:id/{approve,reject}, GET /api/v1/alerts, GET/POST /api/v1/backups, POST /api/v1/backups/:id/restore, GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/skills, GET /api/v1/fleet, GET /api/v1/templates, WS /ws/logs, SSE /sse/status. Routes are thin adapters calling existing business logic.
items.groomingNotes: API layer is the contract between server and web pages — all dashboard pages depend on these endpoints. Routes are thin adapters over existing business logic (no new logic here). Acceptance criteria: all 15 endpoints return {ok, data} or {ok:false, error} shape; streaming endpoints (deploy, build) use SSE correctly; /ws/logs proxies docker logs to browser. SPRINT PAIR: sprint with FEAT-067 (dashboard foundation) — the server scaffold and API layer must land together. The home page (FEAT-067) calls GET /api/v1/status — both must be functional in the same sprint. READY TO SPRINT: no blockers, pairs with FEAT-067.

items.title: Dashboard Doctor page
items.description: Web page calling runChecks() and runFixes(). Table of checks with pass/warn/fail icons. Fix All button triggers POST /api/v1/doctor/fix. Tier A priority — high value from visual interface vs CLI.
items.groomingNotes: Doctor is the hero feature — the visual interface makes it dramatically more accessible than the CLI for Privacy Migrants. Seeing all checks as a table with traffic-light icons and a single 'Fix All' button is the intended UX. Tier A: ship right after dashboard foundation.

items.title: Dashboard Logs page with WebSocket streaming
items.description: Web page using WebSocket /ws/logs. Streams DockerClient.streamLogs() output. Features: category filter, pause/resume, auto-scroll. Adapter pipes child process stdout to WebSocket write function. Tier A priority.
items.groomingNotes: Live log streaming in browser eliminates 'I need terminal access' barrier for fleet operators managing remote deployments. Category filter prevents noise. Tier A: high diagnostic value for any user troubleshooting a misbehaving agent.

items.title: Dashboard Deploy Controls page
items.description: Web page with up/down/restart buttons. Calls deploy{Up,Down,Restart}() via SSE for step progress streaming. Requires adding optional onStep callback to deployUp(), deployDown(), deployRestart() in src/deploy/deploy.ts (backward compatible). Tier A priority.
items.groomingNotes: Deploy controls let Privacy Migrants manage their agent without remembering CLI flags. Up/down/restart with SSE progress streaming matches the quickstart experience (FEAT-062) but from the browser. Requires non-breaking callback addition to deploy.ts. Tier A.

items.title: Dashboard Approvals page
items.description: Web page showing approval queue as card list with approve/reject action buttons. Calls readQueue(), getPending(), approveAction(), rejectAction(). Tier A priority.
items.groomingNotes: Approvals in the browser complete the approval loop for users who are already on the dashboard. Currently approvals come via messaging channel (FEAT-022) — the dashboard is a second approval surface, not a replacement. Tier A: high trust-building feature — users see what the agent wants to do before it acts.

items.title: Dashboard: Skills & Tools management page
items.description: Web page for managing agent capabilities. Skills tab: installed skills list, install from registry/URL, enable/disable, view skill details. Tools tab: installed tools list, tool health status, install/remove tools. Both call existing loadRegistry(), stageSkillInstall(), activateSkill(), removeSkillOp() functions. Card-based layout with install forms.
items.groomingNotes: Tier B dashboard pages expand coverage but CLI is adequate for all four (clawhq doctor, clawhq backup, clawhq skill list, clawhq fleet). Ship after Tier A pages (FEAT-072-075) are validated. Fleet page only valuable when fleet features are actively used.

items.title: Dashboard Init Wizard (web)
items.description: Multi-step web form for agent initialization. Needs ~200 lines of client-side JS for step state + conditional fields + async credential validation. Use htmx hx-vals for form state, server-side session for wizard progress. Tier C — complex, defer until core dashboard is solid.
items.groomingNotes: Complex web form that duplicates clawhq quickstart (FEAT-062). Only valuable for users who can run a browser but not a terminal — that's the 'managed hosting' persona we're deferring to Phase 3. Keep could, revisit when core dashboard is solid and we have non-technical user feedback.

items.title: npm publish setup
items.description: Configure package.json for npm publish: files array (dist/, configs/, README.md, LICENSE), postbuild script (cp -r src/ui/static dist/ui/static), prepublishOnly (npm run build && npm test). Verify with npm pack --dry-run. Ensure npm install -g clawhq works.
items.groomingNotes: SPRINT THIS FIRST — before dashboard work (FEAT-067) begins. Users cannot benefit from the CLI or dashboard until the package is installable. Standalone distribution setup — no code deps, just package.json config. Steps: (1) set files array in package.json: ['dist/', 'configs/', 'README.md', 'LICENSE']; (2) add postbuild script: 'cp -r src/ui/static dist/ui/static' (no-op if dir doesn't exist yet); (3) add prepublishOnly script: 'npm run build && npm test'; (4) set main/bin fields correctly in package.json; (5) run npm pack --dry-run and verify the file list matches exactly dist/, configs/, README.md, LICENSE; (6) install globally with npm install -g . and verify clawhq --version works. Acceptance criteria: npm pack --dry-run shows exactly the right files with no extras (no .env, no src/, no node_modules/); npm install -g . on clean machine works; clawhq --version returns correct version; prepublishOnly runs build + test before publish.

items.title: GitHub Actions CI workflow
items.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (on PR + push to main): three parallel jobs — lint (npm run lint), test (npm test, matrix Node 20+22), typecheck (tsc --noEmit). Fast feedback on every PR.
items.groomingNotes: RETRY NEEDED: Has failed validator twice (failCount: 2). Root cause: the acceptance criteria include 'verify PR shows green/red status checks' which cannot be validated locally — the validator has no GitHub context. REVISED AC FOR VALIDATOR: (1) .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and passes yamllint; (2) trigger block includes 'push: branches: [main]' AND 'pull_request'; (3) three separate jobs named 'lint', 'test', 'typecheck' are defined; (4) test job has strategy.matrix with node-version: [20, 22]; (5) all jobs run npm ci before their main command; (6) no 'needs:' dependencies between jobs (they are parallel). Validator should use 'npx js-yaml .github/workflows/ci.yml' or 'python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/ci.yml"))"' to verify YAML is valid. The ci.yml file already exists and looks structurally correct — the validator should pass on retry with these concrete local ACs.

items.title: GitHub Actions release workflow
items.description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml (on tag v*): validate (full test suite), publish-npm (npm publish with NPM_TOKEN secret), create-release (gh release create with auto-generated notes). Plus scripts/release.sh for version bump + tag + push.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-079 (CI workflow) done. Steps: (1) create .github/workflows/release.yml triggered on push to tags v*; (2) job 'validate': npm ci && npm test && npx tsc --noEmit; (3) job 'publish-npm' (needs: validate): npm ci && npm run build && npm publish using NPM_TOKEN secret; (4) job 'create-release' (needs: validate): gh release create $TAG --generate-notes; (5) create scripts/release.sh: bump version in package.json, git commit, git tag v$VERSION, git push --follow-tags. Validator ACs (local-verifiable): release.yml exists and is valid YAML; trigger is push.tags matching 'v*'; three jobs defined: validate, publish-npm, create-release; publish-npm and create-release both have 'needs: validate'; publish-npm uses secrets.NPM_TOKEN; scripts/release.sh exists and is executable.

items.title: Admin notification dispatcher
items.description: Create src/notifications/ module. NotificationChannel type (webhook/telegram/slack/email) with event subscriptions (alert.critical, alert.warning, approval.pending, health.degraded/recovered, update.available, backup.failed). Storage: ~/.clawhq/notifications.json. CLI: clawhq notify add/list/remove/test. Webhook payloads signed with HMAC-SHA256. Hook into src/alerts/, src/approval/, src/status/, src/update/ for dispatch.
items.groomingNotes: Upgraded to should: Push notifications complete the autopilot experience. The approval queue (FEAT-022, done) already proves the messaging channel pattern — this extends it to system alerts (backup failed, credential expired, health degraded). Without this, the user must actively check the dashboard or run clawhq doctor to discover problems. Telegram integration is the primary channel (matches approval queue UX). Ship after dashboard is stable.

items.title: CLI monolith split (deferred)
items.description: Split src/cli/index.ts (~2655 lines) into src/cli/commands/ modules: plan.ts, build.ts, secure.ts, deploy.ts, operate.ts, evolve.ts, approval.ts, decommission.ts, cloud.ts. Each exports register(program: Command): void. Action handlers use dynamic import() for lazy loading. Index.ts becomes ~80 lines. DEFERRED — only do when index.ts becomes a maintenance problem.
items.groomingNotes: DOWNGRADED must→could: This is internal refactor with zero user-visible value. The title explicitly says '(deferred)'. While code hygiene matters, user-facing items (dashboard, capability layer, memory lifecycle) are higher priority. The organic split is already partially in progress — additional modules (secrets.ts, tool.ts, trace.ts, fleet.ts, etc.) are already committed. Complete the split only when index.ts becomes a genuine maintenance blocker, not on a schedule. Sprint after all must/should user-value items are shipped.

items.title: Cloud auth hooks (deferred)
items.description: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (clawhq login), API key fallback (clawhq login --token), agent registration (clawhq register), cloud status/disconnect. Storage: ~/.clawhq/auth.json (0600 perms). DEFERRED — only build when Tier 3 cloud service is being developed. Until then these would be dead code.
items.groomingNotes: DEFERRED — correct. Cloud auth is dead code until Tier 3 service exists. Don't build the client before the server exists.

items.title: Standalone binary distribution (deferred)
items.description: Node.js SEA with ESM + esbuild bundle → SEA blob → postject injection. 4-platform matrix (linux/darwin × x64/arm64). curl installer script (scripts/install.sh). Checksums in GitHub Release. DEFERRED — ship npm-only first, validate SEA compatibility later.
items.groomingNotes: DEFERRED — npm global install covers the target audience (they have Node.js). SEA binary is a distribution optimization not a user value story. Revisit after npm package is proven.

items.title: Tool execution audit trail — clawhq audit
items.description: clawhq audit shows tool execution history: timestamp, tool name, redacted inputs, summarized outputs. clawhq audit --compliance produces exportable report aligned with OWASP GenAI Top 10 controls. Appends to a local JSONL audit log from within agent activity. Distinct from egress audit (FEAT-059) which covers outbound network calls only — this covers all tool invocations including local tools (tasks, calendar, email). clawhq audit --since <date> and --export flags.
items.groomingNotes: CLARIFICATION: Two separate things exist and both are incomplete for this item. (1) src/cli/trace.ts + src/internal/trace/ — implements 'why did you do that?' decision trace (FEAT covered in PRODUCT.md §7). This is the query/explain side — records DecisionEntry objects to decision-trace.json. NOT a general tool execution audit log. (2) This backlog item (FEAT-085) covers the SEPARATE tool execution audit trail: JSONL log of ALL tool invocations (tasks, calendar, email, etc.) not just decision traces. The writing side does NOT exist yet — no code appends tool invocations to audit.jsonl. Steps needed: (1) create src/audit/ module with types.ts (AuditEntry: timestamp, toolName, redactedInputs, summarizedOutput, sessionId), appender.ts (appendAuditEntry to ~/.clawhq/audit.jsonl), reader.ts (queryAuditLog with since/export filters); (2) hook appender into agent tool execution paths; (3) create src/cli/audit.ts with: audit show (paginated list), audit --since <date>, audit --export (write to file), audit --compliance (OWASP GenAI Top 10 report); (4) register audit command in index.ts. Note: this is could priority — egress audit (FEAT-059 done) covers the higher-stakes case. Only sprint when compliance reporting is a user request. Dependencies: requires hooking into tool execution paths which may need OpenClaw integration surface work.

items.title: Template toolbelts — preconfigured capability bundles
items.description: Expand template system beyond operational profiles. Each template is a complete toolbelt: tools (email/himalaya, calendar/CalDAV, tasks/Todoist, research/Tavily), skills (morning-brief, email-digest), personality/role definition, security posture, and integration wiring. Templates: Assistant (email+calendar+tasks), Researcher (web+notes+files), Coach (messaging+health+goals), Analyst (finance+data+reports), Guardian (security-focused minimal surface), Companion (messaging+memory-heavy). clawhq template use <name> applies the full bundle. clawhq template show <name> previews what gets installed.
items.groomingNotes: CORE PRODUCT PROMISE — blocks 'Replace my X' headline story. Templates are the 'Replace my X' story for Privacy Migrants. Without compelling preconfigured toolbelts, quickstart (FEAT-062) delivers a blank slate not a useful agent. The 6 archetypes (Assistant/Researcher/Coach/Analyst/Guardian/Companion) must map to real replacing decisions. DEPENDENCY: requires FEAT-089 (tool installation Dockerfile patching) to be complete first — a template that installs tools but can't wire them into the container is broken on arrival. Sprint path: FEAT-089 completion → FEAT-086. Acceptance criteria unchanged: clawhq quickstart --template personal-assistant produces an agent immediately useful for email/calendar/tasks with correct tools wired, security posture applied, and skills installed; clawhq template show <name> previews what gets installed before commitment.

items.title: Personality and role system
items.description: Define agent identity beyond config: personality traits, communication style, role boundaries, guardrails. IDENTITY.md generation from template + user customization. Role presets (executive assistant, research analyst, life coach, etc.) that set tone, formality, proactivity level, and domain expertise framing. clawhq role set <preset> or clawhq role customize for interactive editing. Identity files remain read-only at runtime — agent cannot modify its own personality.
items.groomingNotes: PART OF TEMPLATES: Role/personality is delivered inside each template bundle (FEAT-086) — a template IS a personality preset. Standalone clawhq role set/customize is a Tinkerer power feature, not day-1 adoption bloat. Downgraded from must to should. Ship after FEAT-086 delivers baked-in template personalities. dependsOn: FEAT-086. User outcome: Tinkerer can customize agent communication style, formality, and domain framing without editing raw identity files directly.

items.title: Skill registry — discover, install, manage agent behaviors
items.description: Expand skill system into a proper registry. Skills are agent behaviors (meal-planning, meeting-prep, expense-tracking, journaling, workout-planning). clawhq skill search <query> discovers skills from built-in registry + community sources. clawhq skill install <name> with security vetting (sandbox scan, permission review). clawhq skill info <name> shows description, required tools/integrations, permissions. Skill manifest format: name, description, required integrations, cron schedule, scripts, permissions needed.
items.groomingNotes: DISCOVERY NOT BLOCKING: The current clawhq skill install <url|local> pipeline already enables skill addition. A searchable registry is a discovery and community feature — valuable but not blocking the 'Gets more capable over time' design principle, which is served by the install pipeline (FEAT-089) and template skill bundles (FEAT-086). Downgraded from must to should. Ship after FEAT-086 and FEAT-089 are validated. User outcome: user can search and discover community skills by category without knowing the exact URL or name.

items.title: Tool installation framework — add-on capabilities
items.description: Tools are executables the agent can invoke (not skills/behaviors). Categories: communication (email, messaging, voice), productivity (calendar, tasks, notes), data (finance, research, weather), operations (monitoring, secrets, backups, health-checks). clawhq tool install <name> handles: download/generate tool script, add to workspace/tools/, update TOOLS.md, add required env vars to .env template, add Dockerfile fragments for dependencies. clawhq tool list shows installed with health status. clawhq tool check runs credential probes for all installed tools.
items.groomingNotes: PARTIAL IMPL EXISTS: tool.ts (148L) already committed with clawhq tool install/list/remove CLI skeleton. The full acceptance criteria — allowlist validation, Dockerfile patching, and Stage 2 rebuild triggering — needs completion. Sprint should complete these three missing pieces, not start from scratch. CAPABILITY EXPANSION BACKBONE: 'Gets more capable over time' is a core design principle. Without the Dockerfile patching + rebuild trigger, users are stuck with day-1 tools forever. dependsOn: build.ts/two-stage build infrastructure must be functional. Acceptance criteria unchanged: clawhq tool install pandoc patches Stage 2 Dockerfile and triggers rebuild; clawhq tool list shows installed tools with version+health; unlisted packages flagged not silently blocked; clawhq tool remove triggers rebuild and cleans up. READY TO SPRINT: partial impl de-risks this — it's a completion sprint, not a build sprint. Must ship before FEAT-086 (Template toolbelts).

items.title: Service management — backing services for agents
items.description: Manage backing services agents depend on. Services are infrastructure: databases, message queues, vector stores, file storage. clawhq service add postgres/redis/qdrant/minio adds service to docker-compose with proper networking, volumes, health checks. clawhq service list shows running services with health. clawhq service remove <name> with data preservation options. Services auto-wired: env vars injected, firewall rules updated, agent config connected. Start simple: postgres + redis + qdrant (vector search for memory).
items.groomingNotes: INFRASTRUCTURE FOR ADVANCED AGENTS: Backing services (postgres, redis, qdrant) are needed by Tinkerers building sophisticated agents with persistent storage or semantic memory search. Not day-1 for Privacy Migrants. Natural dependency: qdrant is needed by FEAT-092 warm-tier memory search. Ship after Capability Layer core (FEAT-086, FEAT-089) is validated. Acceptance criteria: clawhq service add qdrant adds the service to docker-compose with correct networking/volumes/health checks; env vars auto-injected to agent config; clawhq service list shows running services with health; clawhq service remove preserves data by default with explicit --delete-data flag.

items.title: Integration provider management — swap providers without behavior changes
items.description: Agent talks to 'calendar' not 'Google Calendar'. clawhq integrate add calendar --provider caldav/google/outlook wires the right tool and credentials. clawhq integrate swap calendar --from caldav --to google migrates without changing agent behavior. Provider abstraction layer: each integration category has a standard interface, providers implement it. Categories: email, calendar, tasks, messaging, files, code, finance, research, notes, health, CRM. clawhq integrate list shows active integrations with provider and health.
items.groomingNotes: IMPLEMENTATION FURTHER ALONG THAN NOTES SUGGEST: src/integrate/ module is complete (lifecycle.ts, providers.ts, types.ts) AND src/cli/integrate.ts (401L) is already committed and registered in index.ts. The CLI implements: integrate add <category> (with provider selection, credential setup, firewall update), integrate remove <category>, integrate swap <category> <provider>, integrate list. Sprint should VERIFY + TEST not rebuild: (1) run npm test — verify integrate tests pass; (2) verify integrate add walks through credential validation live; (3) verify swap leaves agent behavior unchanged (same category interface); (4) verify integrate remove tightens egress firewall allowlist; (5) verify integrate list shows health and last-used; (6) check for any TODOs or stubs in lifecycle.ts/providers.ts that are not yet implemented; (7) run clawhq integrate list manually to verify the command works end-to-end. If all ACs pass on review, mark passes:true. If stubs are found, sprint to complete them. Acceptance criteria unchanged from original grooming.

items.title: Memory lifecycle management
items.description: Implement hot→warm→cold memory tier transitions. Hot (≤7 days, ≤100KB, full fidelity in every conversation). Warm (7-90 days, summarized, searchable on demand). Cold (90+ days, compressed, PII masked, archive). clawhq memory status shows tier sizes with usage metrics. clawhq memory compact triggers manual warm/cold transitions. clawhq memory search <query> searches across tiers. Auto-transition via cron. Without management, memory grows ~120KB/day — this prevents bloat while preserving context.
items.groomingNotes: INTERNAL IMPL EXISTS: src/internal/memory/ module is complete with hot/warm/cold tier transitions (transitions.ts), store (store.ts), ingestion (ingestion.ts), reflection (reflection.ts) — all with tests. What is MISSING: the CLI wrapper. Sprint should create the CLI surface only: (1) create src/cli/memory.ts with createMemoryCommand(): Command; (2) memory status subcommand: calls collectMemoryHealth() from internal/memory/store.ts, displays hot/warm/cold tier sizes with entry counts and total bytes, shows growth rate if calculable; (3) memory compact subcommand: calls runAllTransitions() from internal/memory/transitions.ts with fallbackSummarize as the summarizer, reports how many entries were transitioned; (4) register memory command in src/cli/index.ts: import { createMemoryCommand } and program.addCommand(createMemoryCommand()); (5) verify no cron entry is needed (cron auto-compaction can be a follow-up). AC: clawhq memory status shows hot/warm/cold tier sizes; clawhq memory compact triggers transitions and reports count; tsc clean; no regression on existing commands. Note: Phase B (qdrant vector search for clawhq memory search) is a separate future sprint — do not include search in this sprint.

items.title: Dashboard: Fleet management page
items.description: Multi-agent overview page. Table of all configured agents with: name, template, status (running/stopped/degraded), uptime, resource usage, active skills count, pending approvals. Bulk actions: restart all, update all, backup all. Agent drill-down links to per-agent dashboard. Only shown in sidebar if >1 agent configured. Calls collectFleetStatus().
items.groomingNotes: FLEET OPERATOR VALUE: Multi-agent overview page completes the Fleet Operator persona story — they need cross-agent visibility without SSHing into each machine. Conditional display (only shown when >1 agent configured) avoids cluttering single-agent setups. Tier B dashboard page — ship after Tier A pages (FEAT-072-075) are validated. Acceptance criteria: fleet table shows name/template/status/uptime/resource-usage/pending-approvals for all configured agents; bulk restart-all and backup-all actions work; page hidden from sidebar when only one agent exists; agent rows link to per-agent dashboard view.

items.title: Dashboard: Backups & Alerts pages
items.description: Backups page: table of snapshots with date, size, type (full/incremental), create button, restore button with confirmation. Calls listBackups(), createBackup(), restoreBackup(). Alerts page: cards grouped by severity (critical/warning/info) from generateAlerts(). Dismiss/acknowledge actions. Both are Tier B dashboard pages — useful visual upgrade over CLI.
items.groomingNotes: TIER B DASHBOARD PAGES: Both backups and alerts have adequate CLI equivalents (clawhq backup, clawhq doctor). The visual upgrade is real — traffic-light alert cards and a one-click restore button are dramatically more accessible for Privacy Migrants — but these are not blocking adoption when the CLI works. Ship after Tier A dashboard pages (FEAT-072-075) are validated with real users. Acceptance criteria: Backups page shows snapshot table with date/size/type and working create/restore buttons with confirmation dialog; Alerts page shows severity-grouped cards with dismiss/acknowledge actions; both pages use the established dashboard layout from FEAT-067.

_dependsOn_docs: Items may include an optional 'dependsOn' array of item ID strings (e.g. "dependsOn": ["FEAT-001"]). Auto-pick skips items whose dependencies are not yet done.
